proposals.title: Axiology Value Theory
proposals.description: Madhyasth Darshan's theory of value: what values are, how they are recognised and fulfilled, and how mutual satisfaction grounds a humane axiology

proposals.title: Chitta, the Brain, and the Architecture of Memory
proposals.description: Where memory lives in Madhyasth Darshan — chitta and jeevan versus the brain's biological memory — compared with neuroscience and philosophy of mind.

proposals.title: Death Continuity and Rebirth
proposals.description: What happens at death in Madhyasth Darshan: the continuity of jeevan beyond the body and its re-embodiment, compared with Advaita Vedanta's rebirth and the physicalist view that death ends the self

proposals.title: Education and Sanskar
proposals.description: How understanding is formed and carried forward: education, sanskar, study, awakening, and transmission across generations

proposals.title: Family Relationships and Values
proposals.description: The family as the first site of values: recognising relationships and living trust, affection, gratitude, and mutual satisfaction

proposals.title: Free Will Choice and Agency
proposals.description: Whether human conduct is free or determined: definite conduct in the lower orders versus achieved selection at the knowledge order, compared with debates on free will and determinism

proposals.title: God Divinity and the Sacred
proposals.description: How Madhyasth Darshan reframes God and the divine: satta as omnipresence and the awakened (divya) human, compared with theism, Advaita Vedanta's Ishvara, and secular critiques of religion

proposals.title: Governance Justice and Undivided Society
proposals.description: Justice, governance, and law in Madhyasth Darshan: fearlessness, public order, and the institutions of an undivided society

proposals.title: Health Body and Restraint
proposals.description: The jeevan-body relationship in health: ahar-vihar, restraint (sanyam), and well-being as the health-restraint dimension of universal orderliness

proposals.title: Language Meaning and Definition
proposals.description: Madhyasth Darshan's claim that reality is fully expressible: word, meaning, and definition (shabd, artha, Paribhasha), set against Advaita Vedanta's ineffability and modern philosophy of language

proposals.title: Methodology and Hermeneutics
proposals.description: The method behind these studies: reading the primary texts, translation issues, citation discipline, interpretation, and disciplined comparison across traditions

proposals.title: Nature Ecology and Right Use
proposals.description: Living with the other orders of nature: Earth, right-use, regeneration, and ecological balance within coexistence

proposals.title: Philosophy of Mind and Jeevan
proposals.description: How Madhyasth Darshan understands the inner life: jeevan and its faculties — mun, vritti, chitta, buddhi, and atma — and desire, imagination, selection, delusion, and awakening

proposals.title: Prosperity Economics and Right Use
proposals.description: Prosperity without accumulation: production, cyclical economics, right-use of wealth, and the limits of accumulation

proposals.title: Science Technology and Human Purpose
proposals.description: Science, technology, and human purpose: production and convenience, and the limits of instrumental knowledge

proposals.title: Spiritual Practice and Realization
proposals.description: Realization and awakening in Madhyasth Darshan: anubhav, practice, yoga, and liberation from mystery and suffering, compared with moksha traditions

proposals.title: Work Action and Karma
proposals.description: Human action and work in Madhyasth Darshan: effort-motion-result (shram-gati-parinam), karma, labour, and right-use, and how action carries consequence
